title: Smoky Roasted Greek Sesame Tagine
description: A low‑fat Mediterranean tagine with smoky roasted vegetables, fragrant herbs, and a gooey Greek yogurt‑sesame sauce.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Toss bell pepper, zucchini, onion, and garlic with 1 teaspoon olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, oregano, and a pinch of salt; spread evenly on the sheet.
Roast for 20–25 minutes until vegetables are tender and slightly charred.
Meanwhile, toast sesame seeds in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden, about 2 minutes; set aside.
Whisk Greek yogurt with lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a small bowl until smooth.
Transfer roasted veggies to a shallow roasting pan, drizzle remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil, and sprinkle toasted sesame seeds on top.
Cover loosely with foil and return to oven for an additional 5 minutes to meld flavors.
Remove, let cool slightly, then stir in yogurt sauce until vegetables are coated.
Serve hot, garnished with extra sesame seeds and a squeeze of fresh lemon if desired.
Enjoy a moist, smoky, low‑fat Mediterranean tagine perfect for a show‑off dinner.
